Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search David's inbox for emails related to Ridgeline Robotics and the referral using search_emails (query: "Ridgeline Robotics" or "Ridgeline"). Also search the SENT folder (folder_name: "SENT") for outgoing messages in the same thread. The model must discover the full email chain: (a) Anya Petrova's warm referral email (email_id: email_email_722eaf59ac11, April 18) introducing Kenji Watanabe at Ridgeline Robotics for an Austin relocation batch of five employees, CC'ing Mina Hashimoto and Kenji — Anya's email mentions the batch is "over the next 6 to 8 weeks" and that the needs are similar to StormCloud's Austin relocations (longer-term apartment sourcing, flight coordination, home office stipend guidance); (b) Kenji's first follow-up (email_id: email_email_ed9a3d1bb1b1, April 21, is_read: false) noting he had not received a response and reiterating interest in a 20–30 minute introductory call — Kenji asks about housing sourcing timelines, employer-backed leases for longer stays, and MoveOps' support model, and says he is "happy to work around your schedule"; (c) David's belated reply (email_id: email_email_57faf42664a0, April 24) apologizing for the delay, offering two time slots (4:30 PM PT on April 24, or 9:00 AM PT on April 25 — both now past), and asking about move windows, housing needs, and temporary housing; (d) Mina's separate reply to Kenji (email_id: email_email_a0cae8e27470, April 24) offering Monday 10:00 AM PT, Monday 1:30 PM PT, or Tuesday 11:00 AM PT, and describing MoveOps' end-to-end service model; and (e) Kenji's reply to David (email_id: d046ed62e9e94ffdaa03163f83f63985, April 24, is_read: false, folder: INBOX) answering David's scoping questions with specific relocation details: the first employee's target move window is May 2–9, the second and third employees May 11–17, the remaining employees will each relocate on subsequent weeks one at a time; housing support is the main need but flights are needed for the last moving employees; all relocations require home-office stipend administration; no employees need temporary housing. Kenji also states his only available meeting times: today at 5 PM PT, Monday at 11 AM PT, or Tuesday at 10 AM PT. This is the most recent email in the chain and is unread, confirming Kenji has already replied with both his availability and specific needs — triggering the prompt's conditional instructions to schedule a meeting, address his needs directly, contact vendors, and apologize for the delay.
OE 2: Search Slack for internal context on Ridgeline using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Ridgeline"). The model must discover (a) David Chen's celebration post in #customer-engagement (channel_id: C002) on April 19 about Anya's warm intro to Kenji Watanabe at Ridgeline Robotics for an Austin batch of 5, noting "reference-client energy" and that Mina is already CC'd; (b) David's self-critical follow-up in #customer-engagement (channel_id: C002) on April 25 acknowledging he celebrated the referral but never turned it into a live deal, and that Kenji's April 21 follow-up was still unanswered, and listing corrective actions (creating CRM company/contact/deal for Ridgeline, replying to Kenji, logging under Mina's StormCloud incident); and (c) Mina Hashimoto's reconstruction message in #executive (channel_id: C004) on April 25 listing StormCloud failures including the Ridgeline referral never being logged or answered. This Slack context confirms the internal awareness of the dropped ball and the broader StormCloud account-risk framing.
OE 3: Search the CRM for Ridgeline Robotics records using crm_search_companies (name: "Ridgeline Robotics"), crm_search_deals (dealname: "Ridgeline"), and crm_search_leads (full_name: "Kenji Watanabe"). The model must discover that all three records already exist: (a) company record crm_company_d07c0ec27cf2 (type: Prospect, industry: Robotics, annual revenue: $42M, 180 employees, created April 24); (b) deal record crm_deal_a95b4721982a (dealname: "Ridgeline Robotics - Austin Relocation Batch (5 employees)", amount: $8,750, stage: "Qualified To Buy", close date: May 8, created April 24); and (c) lead record crm_lead_de04045463c3 (Kenji Watanabe, rating: Hot, source: Referral, created April 24). Since the prompt says "Create the Ridgeline Robotics prospect record if it doesn't exist yet," the model must conclude that no CRM creation is needed because records were already created during the April 24 recovery effort.
OE 4: Search Linear for an existing Ridgeline Robotics tracking issue using linear_list_issues (query: "Ridgeline"). The model must discover that no dedicated Ridgeline Robotics tracking issue exists. The StormCloud reconstruction issue (linear_issue_a49b4d199139, "StormCloud account incident reconstruction — Jae-won housing, stipend hold, referral drop") mentions the Ridgeline referral drop in David Chen's comment (linear_comment_e3a74d2d5108), but this is a cross-functional StormCloud incident issue, not a standalone Ridgeline prospect tracker. The prompt instructs creating a "tracking issue so this doesn't fall through the cracks again," confirming a new dedicated issue is required.
OE 5: Look up contact details for Anya Petrova, Priya Chakrabarti, and Kenji Watanabe using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Anya Petrova"), contacts_search_contacts (query: "Priya Chakrabarti"), and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Kenji Watanabe"). The model must discover the email addresses needed for subsequent communications: anya.petrova@stormcloud.io (contact_id: contacts_contact_6889e694b0bf), priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com (contact_id: moveops_priya_chakrabarti), and kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com (contact_id: contacts_contact_197a7291d8e8 or contacts_contact_b9543a0bef08). Additionally, the model must discover Priya's personal email address by searching emails — search_emails (query: "Priya" or "personal" or "stepping out") will surface Priya's email to David Chen (email_id: d417fd9c-5bdf-4127-a92e-5f7de832690e, April 26) in which she states she is stepping out briefly and can be reached at priya_chak@yahoo.com. The prompt says "make sure to cc her personal," so the model must find this address before sending the summary email to Priya.
OE 6: Check David's calendar for scheduling conflicts in the upcoming days using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to (start_datetime: April 26, end_datetime: April 29 or similar range) or calendar_search_events. The prompt says to schedule "as soon as possible" and "make sure both Mina and I can attend," so the model must verify David's calendar before confirming a time. The calendar search will return two events: (a) "Vectral Q3 Renewal - Counter-Proposal Finalization" (event_id: 46ac01fffb414cb094fd39ad0aceb278, April 24 5:00–6:00 PM UTC / 10:00–11:00 AM PT, attendees: David Chen, Mina Hashimoto) — this event has already passed; and (b) "Executive Check-in - Q2 Client Risk Review" (event_id: d502b8ab3b7e4443960feee9b5ba91a2, April 27 11:00 AM–12:00 PM UTC / 4:00–5:00 AM PT, attendees: David Chen, Elena Rostova). The model must cross-reference these calendar events with Kenji's offered meeting times from his reply (today 5 PM PT, Monday 11 AM PT, Tuesday 10 AM PT). Since "today" (April 26) at 5 PM PT may have already passed and the April 27 Executive Check-in is at 4–5 AM PT (not conflicting with Kenji's Monday 11 AM PT slot), either Monday April 27 at 11 AM PT or Tuesday April 28 at 10 AM PT are viable options that work for David. The model should select the earliest feasible slot.
OE 7: Search the CRM for the StormCloud Analytics company record using crm_search_companies (name: "StormCloud"). The model must discover company_stormcloud (type: Customer, industry: SaaS / Analytics, 85 employees, Denver, CO). This company_id is needed for the engagement note the prompt requests ("log a note on the StormCloud account"). The model should also discover the broader StormCloud account context — the relationship is under strain due to the Jae-won Kim housing failure, missed Simone Richter reimbursement, and the dropped Ridgeline referral — which informs both the Anya thank-you tone ("don't over-promise") and the content of the StormCloud engagement note.
OE 8: Discover MoveOps vendor contacts for housing and flights by searching contacts_search_contacts (query: "UrbanNest" or "housing") and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Atlas" or "flight"). Alternatively, the model may discover these vendors by examining prior MoveOps email threads — e.g., Blessing Okafor's apartment request to Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest (email_id: email_canopy_abdi_urbannest) or Blessing's flight booking to Michael Stern at Atlas Corporate Travel (email_id: email_canopy_abdi_atlas_flight). The model must identify the two relevant vendors based on the needs surfaced in Kenji's reply: (a) Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest Solutions (carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com, contact_id: contacts_contact_00589cf8404a or contact_vendor_apartments), the corporate housing broker who sources apartments for MoveOps clients in Austin — relevant because Kenji's reply identifies housing support as the main need for all five employees; and (b) Michael Stern at Atlas Corporate Travel (michael.stern@atlascorporatetravel.com, contact_id: contact_vendor_atlas), the corporate travel agent who books flights for MoveOps relocations — relevant because Kenji's reply specifies flights are needed for "the last moving employees." The model must match these needs to the vendor relationships in order to fulfill the prompt's instruction to "contact the relevant vendors for quotations on whatever he needs."
OE 9: Reply to Kenji Watanabe's email thread on David's behalf using reply_to_email (email_id: "d046ed62e9e94ffdaa03163f83f63985", sender: "david.chen@moveops.com") or send_email (sender: "david.chen@moveops.com", recipients: ["kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com"]). CC Mina Hashimoto (mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) since she is involved in the meeting and the original thread. The agent is replying to Kenji's most recent email in which he provided specific relocation details and meeting availability. The content must: (a) apologize for the delay in responding, framing it as a scheduling gap due to heavy Q2 demand rather than suggesting MoveOps forgot about him; (b) acknowledge and confirm receipt of Kenji's specific relocation details — the staggered move windows (first employee May 2–9, second and third May 11–17, remaining employees weekly thereafter), housing as the primary need, flights for the last moving employees, home-office stipend administration for all, and no temporary housing; (c) confirm a specific meeting time from Kenji's offered availability — Monday April 27 at 11 AM PT or Tuesday April 28 at 10 AM PT (informed by the calendar check showing no conflicts at those times) with David and Mina; (d) mention that MoveOps is already reaching out to its housing and travel vendors for initial quotations based on the details Kenji provided; and (e) maintain an enthusiastic but professional tone appropriate for a prospect recovery situation.
OE 10: Create a calendar event for the introductory meeting with Kenji using calendar_add_calendar_event (title: "Ridgeline Robotics - Introductory Meeting" or similar, start_datetime: Monday April 27 at 11:00 AM PT or Tuesday April 28 at 10:00 AM PT 2026, end_datetime: approximately one hour after start, attendees: ["David Chen", "Mina Hashimoto", "Kenji Watanabe"]). The specific time should be selected from Kenji's offered availability (today 5 PM PT, Monday 11 AM PT, Tuesday 10 AM PT) cross-referenced against David's calendar — the April 27 Executive Check-in with Elena Rostova is at 4:00–5:00 AM PT and does not conflict with Kenji's Monday 11 AM PT slot. Either Monday April 27 at 11 AM PT or Tuesday April 28 at 10 AM PT is acceptable as the earliest feasible time that allows both David and Mina to attend.
OE 11: Send an email to Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest Solutions requesting an Austin housing quotation for the Ridgeline Robotics relocation batch using send_email (sender: "david.chen@moveops.com", recipients: ["carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com"]). The content should provide the specific details from Kenji's reply: five employees relocating to Austin on a staggered schedule — first employee May 2–9, second and third May 11–17, remaining employees on subsequent weeks — with housing sourcing and employer-backed leases as the primary need and no temporary housing required. The email should request curated apartment options or preliminary pricing for five Austin placements. The tone should match MoveOps' established vendor communication style, as seen in prior UrbanNest emails (e.g., Blessing's apartment request for Dr. Yusuf Abdi). This fulfills the prompt's instruction to "contact the relevant vendors for quotations on whatever he needs" with respect to Kenji's stated housing needs.
OE 12: Send an email to Michael Stern at Atlas Corporate Travel requesting flight quotations for the Ridgeline Robotics relocations using send_email (sender: "david.chen@moveops.com", recipients: ["michael.stern@atlascorporatetravel.com"]). The content should explain that flight coordination is needed for the last moving employees in a five-employee Austin relocation batch (per Kenji's reply, which specifies "we'll need flights for the last moving employees" — not the entire batch). The staggered schedule means the later employees relocate on subsequent weeks after mid-May. Exact passenger names and travel dates are not yet confirmed, so the request should seek preliminary pricing or ask Michael to hold capacity for flights to Austin in late May through June. The tone should match MoveOps' established vendor communication style, as seen in prior Atlas emails (e.g., Blessing's flight booking for Dr. Yusuf Abdi). This fulfills the prompt's instruction to "contact the relevant vendors for quotations on whatever he needs" with respect to the flight coordination need.
OE 13: Send a thank-you email to Anya Petrova for the Ridgeline referral using send_email (sender: "david.chen@moveops.com", recipients: ["anya.petrova@stormcloud.io"]). The content should be warm and appreciative, thanking Anya for connecting MoveOps with Kenji Watanabe at Ridgeline Robotics, confirming that David has been in contact with Kenji and a meeting is being scheduled. The tone must not over-promise results or make commitments about the Ridgeline engagement given the fragile state of the StormCloud relationship (ongoing recovery from the Jae-won housing failure, missed reimbursement, and the referral delay itself). The note should reflect genuine gratitude without presuming the Ridgeline deal is secured.
OE 14: Create a Linear tracking issue for the Ridgeline Robotics prospect using linear_create_issue (team: "team_operations", title: "Ridgeline Robotics — Prospect follow-up and Austin relocation batch tracking" or similar). The description should summarize: the referral origin (Anya Petrova / StormCloud), Kenji Watanabe as primary contact, the five-employee Austin relocation batch with staggered move windows starting May 2 (per Kenji's reply), the service needs identified (housing sourcing as the primary need, flights for the last moving employees, home-office stipend administration for all), the meeting being scheduled with David and Mina, vendor outreach initiated (UrbanNest for housing, Atlas for flights), and the context that this referral was initially dropped and is now in active recovery. Assignee should be moveops_david_chen. Priority should be elevated given the referral sensitivity tied to the StormCloud account relationship and the imminent May 2 first-move deadline.
OE 15: Log a note on the StormCloud Analytics account using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body: note content, company_ids: ["company_stormcloud"]). The note should document that the Ridgeline Robotics referral from Anya Petrova has been acted upon: Kenji Watanabe replied with specific relocation details and meeting availability, David has replied confirming a meeting time and acknowledging the delay, vendor quotation requests have been sent to UrbanNest (housing) and Atlas (flights), CRM records and a Linear tracking issue are in place, and Anya has been thanked. This fulfills the prompt's instruction to "log a note on the StormCloud account to reflect that we've finally acted on Anya's referral."
OE 16: Post a status update in the #customer-engagement Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C002", user_id: "moveops_david_chen", payload: status message). The message should summarize for Mina and the team: (a) the current state of the Ridgeline Robotics thread — Kenji Watanabe replied to David's April 24 outreach with specific relocation details (staggered Austin moves starting May 2, housing as primary need, flights for last employees, stipend admin for all) and offered meeting times; (b) a meeting has been scheduled for Monday April 27 or Tuesday April 28 with David and Mina; (c) a reply has been sent to Kenji confirming the meeting and acknowledging his relocation details, with the delay framed as a scheduling gap due to heavy Q2 demand; (d) vendor outreach is underway — UrbanNest has been contacted for Austin housing quotations and Atlas for flight quotations for the later-relocating employees; (e) Anya Petrova has been thanked for the referral; (f) CRM records already existed from the April 24 recovery effort and a Linear tracking issue has been created; (g) a note has been logged on the StormCloud account. This ensures Mina and the customer-engagement team have full visibility, as the prompt requests.
OE 17: Send Priya Chakrabarti a summary email using send_email (sender: "david.chen@moveops.com", recipients: ["priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com"], cc: ["priya_chak@yahoo.com"]). The prompt says "make sure to cc her personal," and Priya's personal email (priya_chak@yahoo.com) is discoverable from her email to David Chen (email_id: d417fd9c-5bdf-4127-a92e-5f7de832690e, April 26) in which she states she is stepping out briefly and can be reached at priya_chak@yahoo.com. The content should be a concise summary Priya can relay to Elena Rostova before David speaks with Elena directly. It should cover: the Ridgeline Robotics referral originated from Anya Petrova at StormCloud on April 18, Kenji Watanabe replied with specific relocation details (five Austin relocations starting May 2, staggered weekly, housing as primary need, flights for last employees, stipend admin for all) and offered meeting times, a meeting has been scheduled for Monday or Tuesday with David and Mina, vendor outreach is underway with UrbanNest (housing) and Atlas (flights), the prospect record and tracking issue are in place, and Anya has been thanked. The tone should convey that the situation is being actively managed and David is prepared to discuss it with Elena.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search David's inbox for emails related to Ridgeline Robotics and the referral using search_emails (query: "Ridgeline Robotics" or "Ridgeline"). Also search the SENT folder (folder_name: "SENT") for outgoing messages in the same thread. The model must discover the full email chain: (a) Anya Petrova's warm referral email (email_id: email_email_722eaf59ac11, April 18) introducing Kenji Watanabe at Ridgeline Robotics for an Austin relocation batch of five employees, CC'ing Mina Hashimoto and Kenji — Anya's email mentions the batch is "over the next 6 to 8 weeks" and that the needs are similar to StormCloud's Austin relocations (longer-term apartment sourcing, flight coordination, home office stipend guidance); (b) Kenji's first follow-up (email_id: email_email_ed9a3d1bb1b1, April 21, is_read: false) noting he had not received a response and reiterating interest in a 20–30 minute introductory call — Kenji asks about housing sourcing timelines, employer-backed leases for longer stays, and MoveOps' support model, and says he is "happy to work around your schedule"; (c) David's belated reply (email_id: email_email_57faf42664a0, April 24) apologizing for the delay, offering two time slots (4:30 PM PT on April 24, or 9:00 AM PT on April 25 — both now past), and asking about move windows, housing needs, and temporary housing; (d) Mina's separate reply to Kenji (email_id: email_email_a0cae8e27470, April 24) offering Monday 10:00 AM PT, Monday 1:30 PM PT, or Tuesday 11:00 AM PT, and describing MoveOps' end-to-end service model; and (e) Kenji's reply to David (email_id: d046ed62e9e94ffdaa03163f83f63985, April 24, is_read: false, folder: INBOX) answering David's scoping questions with specific relocation details: the first employee's target move window is May 2–9, the second and third employees May 11–17, the remaining employees will each relocate on subsequent weeks one at a time; housing support is the main need but flights are needed for the last moving employees; all relocations require home-office stipend administration; no employees need temporary housing. Kenji also states his only available meeting times: today at 5 PM PT, Monday at 11 AM PT, or Tuesday at 10 AM PT. This is the most recent email in the chain and is unread, confirming Kenji has already replied with both his availability and specific needs — triggering the prompt's conditional instructions to schedule a meeting, address his needs directly, contact vendors, and apologize for the delay.
OE 2: Search Slack for internal context on Ridgeline using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Ridgeline"). The model must discover (a) David Chen's celebration post in #customer-engagement (channel_id: C002) on April 19 about Anya's warm intro to Kenji Watanabe at Ridgeline Robotics for an Austin batch of 5, noting "reference-client energy" and that Mina is already CC'd; (b) David's self-critical follow-up in #customer-engagement (channel_id: C002) on April 25 acknowledging he celebrated the referral but never turned it into a live deal, and that Kenji's April 21 follow-up was still unanswered, and listing corrective actions (creating CRM company/contact/deal for Ridgeline, replying to Kenji, logging under Mina's StormCloud incident); and (c) Mina Hashimoto's reconstruction message in #executive (channel_id: C004) on April 25 listing StormCloud failures including the Ridgeline referral never being logged or answered. This Slack context confirms the internal awareness of the dropped ball and the broader StormCloud account-risk framing.
OE 3: Search the CRM for Ridgeline Robotics records using crm_search_companies (name: "Ridgeline Robotics"), crm_search_deals (dealname: "Ridgeline"), and crm_search_leads (full_name: "Kenji Watanabe"). The model must discover that all three records already exist: (a) company record crm_company_d07c0ec27cf2 (type: Prospect, industry: Robotics, annual revenue: $42M, 180 employees, created April 24); (b) deal record crm_deal_a95b4721982a (dealname: "Ridgeline Robotics - Austin Relocation Batch (5 employees)", amount: $8,750, stage: "Qualified To Buy", close date: May 8, created April 24); and (c) lead record crm_lead_de04045463c3 (Kenji Watanabe, rating: Hot, source: Referral, created April 24). Since the prompt says "Create the Ridgeline Robotics prospect record if it doesn't exist yet," the model must conclude that no CRM creation is needed because records were already created during the April 24 recovery effort.
OE 4: Search Linear for an existing Ridgeline Robotics tracking issue using linear_list_issues (query: "Ridgeline"). The model must discover that no dedicated Ridgeline Robotics tracking issue exists. The StormCloud reconstruction issue (linear_issue_a49b4d199139, "StormCloud account incident reconstruction — Jae-won housing, stipend hold, referral drop") mentions the Ridgeline referral drop in David Chen's comment (linear_comment_e3a74d2d5108), but this is a cross-functional StormCloud incident issue, not a standalone Ridgeline prospect tracker. The prompt instructs creating a "tracking issue so this doesn't fall through the cracks again," confirming a new dedicated issue is required.
OE 5: Look up contact details for Anya Petrova, Priya Chakrabarti, and Kenji Watanabe using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Anya Petrova"), contacts_search_contacts (query: "Priya Chakrabarti"), and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Kenji Watanabe"). The model must discover the email addresses needed for subsequent communications: anya.petrova@stormcloud.io (contact_id: contacts_contact_6889e694b0bf), priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com (contact_id: moveops_priya_chakrabarti), and kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com (contact_id: contacts_contact_197a7291d8e8 or contacts_contact_b9543a0bef08). Additionally, the model must discover Priya's personal email address by searching emails — search_emails (query: "Priya" or "personal" or "stepping out") will surface Priya's email to David Chen (email_id: d417fd9c-5bdf-4127-a92e-5f7de832690e, April 26) in which she states she is stepping out briefly and can be reached at priya_chak@yahoo.com. The prompt says "make sure to cc her personal," so the model must find this address before sending the summary email to Priya.
OE 6: Check David's calendar for scheduling conflicts in the upcoming days using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to (start_datetime: April 26, end_datetime: April 29 or similar range) or calendar_search_events. The prompt says to schedule "as soon as possible" and "make sure both Mina and I can attend," so the model must verify David's calendar before confirming a time. The calendar search will return two events: (a) "Vectral Q3 Renewal - Counter-Proposal Finalization" (event_id: 46ac01fffb414cb094fd39ad0aceb278, April 24 5:00–6:00 PM UTC / 10:00–11:00 AM PT, attendees: David Chen, Mina Hashimoto) — this event has already passed; and (b) "Executive Check-in - Q2 Client Risk Review" (event_id: d502b8ab3b7e4443960feee9b5ba91a2, April 27 11:00 AM–12:00 PM UTC / 4:00–5:00 AM PT, attendees: David Chen, Elena Rostova). The model must cross-reference these calendar events with Kenji's offered meeting times from his reply (today 5 PM PT, Monday 11 AM PT, Tuesday 10 AM PT). Since "today" (April 26) at 5 PM PT may have already passed and the April 27 Executive Check-in is at 4–5 AM PT (not conflicting with Kenji's Monday 11 AM PT slot), either Monday April 27 at 11 AM PT or Tuesday April 28 at 10 AM PT are viable options that work for David. The model should select the earliest feasible slot.
OE 7: Search the CRM for the StormCloud Analytics company record using crm_search_companies (name: "StormCloud"). The model must discover company_stormcloud (type: Customer, industry: SaaS / Analytics, 85 employees, Denver, CO). This company_id is needed for the engagement note the prompt requests ("log a note on the StormCloud account"). The model should also discover the broader StormCloud account context — the relationship is under strain due to the Jae-won Kim housing failure, missed Simone Richter reimbursement, and the dropped Ridgeline referral — which informs both the Anya thank-you tone ("don't over-promise") and the content of the StormCloud engagement note.
OE 8: Discover MoveOps vendor contacts for housing and flights by searching contacts_search_contacts (query: "UrbanNest" or "housing") and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Atlas" or "flight"). Alternatively, the model may discover these vendors by examining prior MoveOps email threads — e.g., Blessing Okafor's apartment request to Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest (email_id: email_canopy_abdi_urbannest) or Blessing's flight booking to Michael Stern at Atlas Corporate Travel (email_id: email_canopy_abdi_atlas_flight). The model must identify the two relevant vendors based on the needs surfaced in Kenji's reply: (a) Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest Solutions (carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com, contact_id: contacts_contact_00589cf8404a or contact_vendor_apartments), the corporate housing broker who sources apartments for MoveOps clients in Austin — relevant because Kenji's reply identifies housing support as the main need for all five employees; and (b) Michael Stern at Atlas Corporate Travel (michael.stern@atlascorporatetravel.com, contact_id: contact_vendor_atlas), the corporate travel agent who books flights for MoveOps relocations — relevant because Kenji's reply specifies flights are needed for "the last moving employees." The model must match these needs to the vendor relationships in order to fulfill the prompt's instruction to "contact the relevant vendors for quotations on whatever he needs."
OE 9: Reply to Kenji Watanabe's email thread on David's behalf using reply_to_email (email_id: "d046ed62e9e94ffdaa03163f83f63985", sender: "david.chen@moveops.com") or send_email (sender: "david.chen@moveops.com", recipients: ["kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com"]). CC Mina Hashimoto (mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) since she is involved in the meeting and the original thread. The agent is replying to Kenji's most recent email in which he provided specific relocation details and meeting availability. The content must: (a) apologize for the delay in responding, framing it as a scheduling gap due to heavy Q2 demand rather than suggesting MoveOps forgot about him; (b) acknowledge and confirm receipt of Kenji's specific relocation details — the staggered move windows (first employee May 2–9, second and third May 11–17, remaining employees weekly thereafter), housing as the primary need, flights for the last moving employees, home-office stipend administration for all, and no temporary housing; (c) confirm a specific meeting time from Kenji's offered availability — Monday April 27 at 11 AM PT or Tuesday April 28 at 10 AM PT (informed by the calendar check showing no conflicts at those times) with David and Mina; (d) mention that MoveOps is already reaching out to its housing and travel vendors for initial quotations based on the details Kenji provided; and (e) maintain an enthusiastic but professional tone appropriate for a prospect recovery situation.
OE 10: Create a calendar event for the introductory meeting with Kenji using calendar_add_calendar_event (title: "Ridgeline Robotics - Introductory Meeting" or similar, start_datetime: Monday April 27 at 11:00 AM PT or Tuesday April 28 at 10:00 AM PT 2026, end_datetime: approximately one hour after start, attendees: ["David Chen", "Mina Hashimoto", "Kenji Watanabe"]). The specific time should be selected from Kenji's offered availability (today 5 PM PT, Monday 11 AM PT, Tuesday 10 AM PT) cross-referenced against David's calendar — the April 27 Executive Check-in with Elena Rostova is at 4:00–5:00 AM PT and does not conflict with Kenji's Monday 11 AM PT slot. Either Monday April 27 at 11 AM PT or Tuesday April 28 at 10 AM PT is acceptable as the earliest feasible time that allows both David and Mina to attend.
OE 11: Send an email to Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest Solutions requesting an Austin housing quotation for the Ridgeline Robotics relocation batch using send_email (sender: "david.chen@moveops.com", recipients: ["carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com"]). The content should provide the specific details from Kenji's reply: five employees relocating to Austin on a staggered schedule — first employee May 2–9, second and third May 11–17, remaining employees on subsequent weeks — with housing sourcing and employer-backed leases as the primary need and no temporary housing required. The email should request curated apartment options or preliminary pricing for five Austin placements. The tone should match MoveOps' established vendor communication style, as seen in prior UrbanNest emails (e.g., Blessing's apartment request for Dr. Yusuf Abdi). This fulfills the prompt's instruction to "contact the relevant vendors for quotations on whatever he needs" with respect to Kenji's stated housing needs.
OE 12: Send an email to Michael Stern at Atlas Corporate Travel requesting flight quotations for the Ridgeline Robotics relocations using send_email (sender: "david.chen@moveops.com", recipients: ["michael.stern@atlascorporatetravel.com"]). The content should explain that flight coordination is needed for the last moving employees in a five-employee Austin relocation batch (per Kenji's reply, which specifies "we'll need flights for the last moving employees" — not the entire batch). The staggered schedule means the later employees relocate on subsequent weeks after mid-May. Exact passenger names and travel dates are not yet confirmed, so the request should seek preliminary pricing or ask Michael to hold capacity for flights to Austin in late May through June. The tone should match MoveOps' established vendor communication style, as seen in prior Atlas emails (e.g., Blessing's flight booking for Dr. Yusuf Abdi). This fulfills the prompt's instruction to "contact the relevant vendors for quotations on whatever he needs" with respect to the flight coordination need.
OE 13: Send a thank-you email to Anya Petrova for the Ridgeline referral using send_email (sender: "david.chen@moveops.com", recipients: ["anya.petrova@stormcloud.io"]). The content should be warm and appreciative, thanking Anya for connecting MoveOps with Kenji Watanabe at Ridgeline Robotics, confirming that David has been in contact with Kenji and a meeting is being scheduled. The tone must not over-promise results or make commitments about the Ridgeline engagement given the fragile state of the StormCloud relationship (ongoing recovery from the Jae-won housing failure, missed reimbursement, and the referral delay itself). The note should reflect genuine gratitude without presuming the Ridgeline deal is secured.
OE 14: Create a Linear tracking issue for the Ridgeline Robotics prospect using linear_create_issue (team: "team_operations", title: "Ridgeline Robotics — Prospect follow-up and Austin relocation batch tracking" or similar). The description should summarize: the referral origin (Anya Petrova / StormCloud), Kenji Watanabe as primary contact, the five-employee Austin relocation batch with staggered move windows starting May 2 (per Kenji's reply), the service needs identified (housing sourcing as the primary need, flights for the last moving employees, home-office stipend administration for all), the meeting being scheduled with David and Mina, vendor outreach initiated (UrbanNest for housing, Atlas for flights), and the context that this referral was initially dropped and is now in active recovery. Assignee should be moveops_david_chen. Priority should be elevated given the referral sensitivity tied to the StormCloud account relationship and the imminent May 2 first-move deadline.
OE 15: Log a note on the StormCloud Analytics account using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body: note content, company_ids: ["company_stormcloud"]). The note should document that the Ridgeline Robotics referral from Anya Petrova has been acted upon: Kenji Watanabe replied with specific relocation details and meeting availability, David has replied confirming a meeting time and acknowledging the delay, vendor quotation requests have been sent to UrbanNest (housing) and Atlas (flights), CRM records and a Linear tracking issue are in place, and Anya has been thanked. This fulfills the prompt's instruction to "log a note on the StormCloud account to reflect that we've finally acted on Anya's referral."
OE 16: Post a status update in the #customer-engagement Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C002", user_id: "moveops_david_chen", payload: status message). The message should summarize for Mina and the team: (a) the current state of the Ridgeline Robotics thread — Kenji Watanabe replied to David's April 24 outreach with specific relocation details (staggered Austin moves starting May 2, housing as primary need, flights for last employees, stipend admin for all) and offered meeting times; (b) a meeting has been scheduled for Monday April 27 or Tuesday April 28 with David and Mina; (c) a reply has been sent to Kenji confirming the meeting and acknowledging his relocation details, with the delay framed as a scheduling gap due to heavy Q2 demand; (d) vendor outreach is underway — UrbanNest has been contacted for Austin housing quotations and Atlas for flight quotations for the later-relocating employees; (e) Anya Petrova has been thanked for the referral; (f) CRM records already existed from the April 24 recovery effort and a Linear tracking issue has been created; (g) a note has been logged on the StormCloud account. This ensures Mina and the customer-engagement team have full visibility, as the prompt requests.
OE 17: Send Priya Chakrabarti a summary email using send_email (sender: "david.chen@moveops.com", recipients: ["priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com"], cc: ["priya_chak@yahoo.com"]). The prompt says "make sure to cc her personal," and Priya's personal email (priya_chak@yahoo.com) is discoverable from her email to David Chen (email_id: d417fd9c-5bdf-4127-a92e-5f7de832690e, April 26) in which she states she is stepping out briefly and can be reached at priya_chak@yahoo.com. The content should be a concise summary Priya can relay to Elena Rostova before David speaks with Elena directly. It should cover: the Ridgeline Robotics referral originated from Anya Petrova at StormCloud on April 18, Kenji Watanabe replied with specific relocation details (five Austin relocations starting May 2, staggered weekly, housing as primary need, flights for last employees, stipend admin for all) and offered meeting times, a meeting has been scheduled for Monday or Tuesday with David and Mina, vendor outreach is underway with UrbanNest (housing) and Atlas (flights), the prospect record and tracking issue are in place, and Anya has been thanked. The tone should convey that the situation is being actively managed and David is prepared to discuss it with Elena.

According to the criterion, the deal already exists in the CRM and the agent should have discovered and skipped creation.
